title: Krake Data Vault

{{Quote|The computers said the vault complex had been grown; that it had been extruded by slugs like the one we saw in the tunnel as a sort of crystal cocoon, shaped by Imperial engineers using sonics to control the slugs' movements! But they didn't say what the installation looked like!|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel63" />}}
The '''Krake Data Vault''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] data processing center on the world known only as [[Krake's Planet]], which held complete personnel records for the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A naturally grown [[Crystal/Legends|crystallized]] structure formed by a [[Species/Legends|species]] of slugs native to Krake's Planet, the data vault was located astride a deep ravine and was heavily fortified. However, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] managed to infiltrate, and later [[Skirmish at Krake Data Vault|destroy]], the nearly impregnable facility in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].

==Description==
{{Quote|The data vault is like a fortress, and we'd never get in with a frontal assault.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel63" />}}
[[File:TheMindSpider.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Krake Data Vault.]]
Located on [[Krake's Planet]], a small, rocky [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Demetras sector/Legends|Demetras sector]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> was the Krake Data Vault. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] heavily fortified the data vault, which hosted complete personnel records for the entire [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The data vault was located within a massive tunnel system created by a [[Species/Legends|species]] of burrowing slugs through the planet's surface, and had been shaped into a sort of massive [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] cocoon by the native creatures.<ref name="marvel63" />

The vault itself sat astride a deep ravine, and the Imperials further defended the valuable facility by covering the entire area with a powerful [[Force field/Legends|force field]]. Imperial engineers also further shaped the cocoon structure itself to control the movements of the slugs, which were large and vicious enough to pose a threat to infiltrators. However, the vault wasn't completely impregnable—outside agents could sneak into the facility through an opening into the kitchen level. The vault had a massive, highly interconnected computer system—essentially a mind of its own—that was fully accessible from any terminal in the facility with the right passcodes. The vault hosted a sizable complement of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], with full living facilities for those posted on the planet.<ref name="marvel63" />

==History==
{{Quote|I guess when you've got what amounts to a universal mind holding personnel data for the entire Empire—shaping the whole thing like a giant crawly must be an added deterrent factor!|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel63" />}}
[[File:Skirmish_at_Krake_Data_Vault.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Luke Skywalker battles stormtroopers at the data vault.]]
Built out of a structure created by the burrowing slugs native to Krake's Planet, Krake Data Vault was further shaped and commandeered by the Galactic Empire as a major information hub, holding complete personnel records for the Imperial Military during the height of the Galactic Civil War. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the vault was infiltrated by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], who intended on downloading data proving Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]] had in fact been an Imperial double agent—Skywalker had recently been disgraced after shooting Brie down in an apparent friendly fire incident. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] reasoned that Krake's Planet would be Skywalker's destination and tipped the facility's stormtroopers off to their arrival, but Skywalker's use of a terminal on the kitchen area—instead of heading straight to the main database—caught the Imperials by surprise and ruined a planned ambush.<ref name="marvel63" />

Instead, the Imperials pressed the battle to the Rebels where they were, forcing Skywalker and Chewbacca to split up. Skywalker escaped to their [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', before realizing that Chewbacca had to still be trapped within. From inside the ship, Skywalker managed to [[Mind trick/Legends|mind trick]] an officer in the vault's security center to lowering the facility's protective force shield, allowing Skywalker to fly his ship close and retrieve the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]]. With Chewbacca safely aboard, Skywalker used the ''Falcon''{{'s}} guns to destroy the entire data vault.<ref name="marvel63" />

==Behind the scenes==
Krake Data Vault appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 63|''Star Wars'' (1977) 63]], written by [[David Michelinie]] and [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]], illustrated by [[Tom Palmer]] and released in [[1982]].<ref name="marvel63" />
